title: Juhl coefficient-system scaffold
summary: Defines abstract direct and inverse coefficient families and basic composition statistics, but the advertised closed coefficients m_I and n_I are currently placeholders equal to one.

mathematicalContent.heading: Abstract coefficient families
mathematicalContent.body: A coefficient system assigns two rational coefficients m_I and n_I to every ordered composition, allowing direct and inverse Juhl formulas to use different weights.

mathematicalContent.heading: Composition statistics
mathematicalContent.body: The module records partial sums, the product of parts, and the product of factorials of parts for later closed formulas.

mathematicalContent.heading: Current placeholder
mathematicalContent.body: Both mCoeffQ and nCoeffQ ignore I and return 1. The resulting singleton normalization theorem is therefore definitional and is not the actual general Juhl coefficient formula.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores two rational coefficient families m_I and n_I on all ordered compositions.
Defines singleton normalization for the inverse coefficients.

correspondence.leanType: def pairNCoeff (C : CoefficientSystem) (a b : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : 0 < b) : ℚ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Returns the inverse coefficient attached to the two-part composition (a,b).

correspondence.leanType: def partialSums {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : List ℕ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Computes the scan of initial sums, beginning with zero.

correspondence.leanType: def partsProdQ {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : ℚ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multiplies the composition parts after casting to ℚ.

correspondence.leanType: def factorialPartsProdQ {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : ℚ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multiplies the factorials of all composition parts in ℚ.

correspondence.leanType: def mCoeffQ {N : ℕ} (_I : Composition N) : ℚ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Current placeholder for the closed Juhl m-coefficient; it returns one for every composition.

correspondence.leanType: def nCoeffQ {N : ℕ} (_I : Composition N) : ℚ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Current placeholder for the closed Juhl n-coefficient; it returns one for every composition.
Packages the two constant-one placeholder families as a coefficient system.

correspondence.leanType: theorem nCoeffQ_singleton (N : ℕ) (hN : 0 < N) : nCoeffQ (Composition.singleton N hN) = 1
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves the singleton n-coefficient is one by unfolding the placeholder.
Proves singleton normalization for the constant-one placeholder system.

references.note: Source for ordered GJMS compositions and coefficients.

references.locator: Direct ambient proof of Juhl's formulas
references.note: Ambient interpretation of the composition formula.

limitations: The exact Juhl product normalizations have not been implemented: mCoeffQ and nCoeffQ are literally constant 1. Accordingly, closedCoefficientSystem and its normalization theorem must not be presented as the genuine closed coefficient theorem.
